enter.speaker: narrator
enter.text: The figure sits on a flat stone, motionless as the dead trees around him. His robes are the color of the water. Only his eyes move when you approach — sharp, unhurried, and far too awake for a place like this.

hello.speaker: hermit
hello.text: You came from the west. Through Ashenvale, past the king's corpse. I felt the crown break from here. The marsh shuddered. Everything did.

choices.text: What lies beyond the eastern mist?

place.speaker: hermit
place.text: Ashenmere. Old name for old water. The bog swallowed a village once — you can still see the rooftops when the water is low and the light is wrong. Things live down there. Patient things.

east.speaker: hermit
east.text: Beyond the mist? Something older than the Hollow King. He was a symptom. The sickness runs deeper — into stone, into root. You will see, when the path opens. It is not ready for you yet. Or you for it.

why.speaker: hermit
why.text: Someone must watch the water. The things beneath it do not sleep, but they are slow, and a careful man can keep them slow. I have been careful for a very long time. It is not a life I would recommend.

leave.speaker: hermit
leave.text: Walk softly. The marsh remembers footsteps.
